title: Chionanthus sutepensis
intro: Chionanthus sutepensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, commonly known as a type of olive tree. The plant has been traditionally used in Thai medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits in reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chionanthus sutepensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Thai herbal medicine, Chionanthus sutepensis is often prepared as a decoction or infused as a tea, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and infusions for promoting overall well-being and health.
